219	## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
220	
221	1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
222	2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
223	3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
224	4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
225	5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
226	6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile. **Partially fixed 2026-05-28 (Claim B):** Console 3 inline modals, `RestorePreviewModal.tsx`, `TabClosePrompt.tsx`, `IographicExportDialog.tsx` migrated to Dialog wrapper (Radix provides ARIA automatically). Remaining: `Import` Modal/Drawer, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal`, designer wizard dialogs.
227	7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29). **Fixed 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b):** All tokens enumerated above are now defined — Console: `--io-bg` (A1), `--io-text` (A2); Designer: `--io-surface-hover` (A3), `--io-font-sans` (A4), `--io-error` (A6), `--io-surface-raised` (A7); `--io-accent-muted` not defined — single consumer `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` updated to use `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (A8); Settings: `--io-text-on-accent` (A5), `--io-overlay` (A9), `--io-accent-rgb` (A10). As of 2026-05-27, zero undefined token references exist among the tokens surveyed in this audit.
228	8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
229	9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
230	10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
231	11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-z-modal` token raised from 300 to 1000; full z-index scale set (A13: dropdown:500, modal:1000, command:1200, visual-lock:1500, kiosk-auth:1800, toast:2000, emergency:3000). **Partially fixed 2026-05-28 (Claim B):** `ConfirmDialog.tsx` patched to `var(--io-z-modal)` / `calc(var(--io-z-modal) + 1)`; new `Dialog.tsx` consumers use `var(--io-modal-backdrop)`. Remaining hardcoded z-index values in unmigrated dialogs still uncoordinated — full migration deferred to consumer migration pass.
232	12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.
233	
234	---
235	
236	## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on
237	
238	1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.
239	
240	2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.
241	
242	3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.
243	
244	4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.
245	
246	5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.
247	
248	6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.
249	
250	7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
251	
252	---
253	
254	## Reconciliation Log
255	
256	*Per-module audit files (01-console.md, 01-designer.md, 01-settings.md) not updated; 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections below apply to 02-comparison.md only.*
257	
258	### Original Discrepancies — 03-verification.md
259	
260	1. **Cat 1, Notes, Designer** — Removed "fully hardcoded" claim for SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS; two of six entries use registered tokens (`SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"`, `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #1 (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55).
261	
262	2. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Settings** — Removed "uppercase/0.05em" from labelStyle description; settingsStyles.ts:15-21 has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. Replaced with `marginBottom: 5px` and file citation. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #2.
263	
264	3. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Designer** — Removed "(unregistered token)" from `--io-input-bg`; added `--io-input-border` entry with registration citations (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 (ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367).
265	
266	4. **Cat 7, Deviations, Designer** — Replaced "appear to be unregistered" for `--io-input-bg`/`--io-input-border` with confirmed-registered status and line references (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3.
267	
268	5. **Cat 7, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-space-*` appear unregistered" with confirmation that 17 tokens are defined at index.css:148-164. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #4.
269	
270	6. **Cat 7, Implementation, Shared column** — Changed "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" to note that Designer's ShapePointSelector uses two of the five (`--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / List 1 Item 7 correction.
271	
272	7. **Cat 7, Deviations, Shared column** — Changed "All three: none use `--io-input-*` tokens" to accurate per-module split (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / Cat 7 table rows 4 and 8.
273	
274	8. **Cat 8, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-info` undefined" with confirmation that `--io-info: #3b82f6` is defined at index.css:72; fallback equals token value, no visual regression. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #5 (AuthProviders.tsx:528).
275	
276	9. **Cat 10, Notes, Designer** — Replaced "`io-multiselect-active` has no observable CSS rule" with confirmation that rule exists at index.css:904-909 (drop-shadow filter on selected nodes during multiselect). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #6.
277	
278	10. **Cat 11, Visual Properties, Settings** — Changed `--io-modal-backdrop` label from "(undefined)" to "(defined: `var(--io-surface-overlay)` at index.css:139)". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8.
279	
280	11. **Cat 11, Deviations, Settings** — Removed false "undefined" claim for `--io-modal-backdrop`; noted it is defined and used in ≥6 Settings files; corrected `window.confirm()` count from 4 to 8 with file breakdown (OpcSources:3, Import:3, CameraStreams:1, SupplementalConnectorsTab:1). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #7 and #8.
281	
282	12. **Cat 11, Notes, Shared column** — Changed "All three: `--io-modal-backdrop` and `--io-modal-bg` tokens are not used" to accurately note `--io-modal-backdrop` IS used in ≥6 Settings files; only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8 / List 1 Item 11 correction.
283	
284	13. **Cat 11, Implementation, Settings** — Updated `window.confirm()` count in implementation row from "4" to "8". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #7.
285	
286	14. **List 1, Item 7** — Changed "All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-*` tokens" to partial-use statement (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 7 correction.
287	
288	15. **List 1, Item 11** — Changed "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`" to accurate statement: only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 11 correction.
289	
290	16. **List 2, Item 7, Settings undefined tokens** — Removed four tokens from Settings undefined list that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (discrepancy #8, index.css:139), `--io-info` (discrepancy #9, index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (discrepancy #10, index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (discrepancy #11, index.css:29). Retained genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #8–#11.
291	
292	### Additional Discrepancies — 03c-pre-reconciliation.md, Section 3
293	
294	17. **03c Discrepancy A — `--io-surface-sunken`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:26. Reviewed 02-comparison.md — no hedged language about `--io-surface-sunken` was present in the comparison file; it was already treated as a valid token. No text change to 02. The underlying inconsistency finding (Cat 5, Deviations, Designer: equipment tiles use `--io-surface-sunken` vs `--io-surface-elevated` for other tiles) remains a valid real inconsistency in surface-tier choice. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy A.
295	
296	18. **03c Discrepancy B — `--io-accent-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:42 (`rgba(45,212,191,0.1)`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-accent-subtle` does not appear in the comparison file; hedged claims were only in 01-designer.md. No text change to 02. The CanvasLayerRow indigo fallback (`rgba(99,102,241,0.1)`) is dead code with a wrong hue — latent but not breaking. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy B.
297	
298	19. **03c Discrepancy C — `--io-border-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:46 (`#27272a`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-border-subtle` appears in Cat 1 Visual Properties (Settings) already treated as a valid token reference. No hedged language present. No text change to 02. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy C.
299	
300	### Shared Infrastructure Additions — 03c Section 2 + 03-verification.md Missing-from-Comparison
301	
302	20. **Category 5 Shared Infrastructure — selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx (full entry)**: Added five-field entry covering the `var(--accent)` prefix bug (undefined token; should be `var(--io-accent)`), invisible selection overlay, and wrong hardcoded marquee background color (`rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` vs `var(--io-accent-subtle)`). Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #1; 03c Sections 2.1 and 2.2.
303	
304	21. **Category 8 Shared Infrastructure — alarmFlash.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry covering hardcoded alarm hex colors (values match dark-theme tokens but do not adapt to light/HPHMI themes). Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #2; 03c Section 2.3.
305	
306	22. **Category 8 Shared Infrastructure — operationalState.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry documenting intentional ISA-101 hardcoded colors with `!important` as a documented design exception — not a gap to close. Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #3; 03c Section 2.4.
307	
308	23. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 5). Sources: same as #20; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 secondary.
309	
310	24. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — alarmFlash.css (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 8). Sources: same as #21; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 secondary.
311	
312	25. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — operationalState.css (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 8). Sources: same as #22; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 primary, Cat 8 secondary.
313	
314	26. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — lod.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry for structural-only CSS with no deviations; recorded for completeness per instruction. Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #4; 03c Section 2.5 (category assignment: Cat 10 exclusively).
315	
316	### Meta
317	
318	27. **Per-module audit files not updated**: 01-console.md, 01-designer.md, and 01-settings.md retain their original (some incorrect) content. 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections in this log apply to 02-comparison.md only.

# UI Audit Comparison — Cross-Module Analysis

**Source files:** `ui-audit/01-console.md`, `ui-audit/01-designer.md`, `ui-audit/01-settings.md`
**Date:** 2026-05-27

---

## Category 1 — Color Palette and Theme Tokens

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Shared-component; all colors via CSS custom properties from `index.css`; no module-specific token overrides | Inline-styles; all color references via CSS custom properties from app-shell token registry; no module-specific token declarations | Inline-styles; all color references are CSS custom property calls into app-shell token set defined in `index.css`; no module-specific token definitions | All three: primary color infrastructure is the 138-token registry in `index.css`; no module owns its own token namespace | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:19–295` (138 tokens); hardcoded values in `AlarmListPane.tsx`, `PointTablePane.tsx`, `TrendPane.tsx`, `index.tsx` | `index.css:19–219`; `shared/theme/tokens.ts` (JS mirror); hardcoded overrides in `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar`, `DesignerGraphicsList` | `index.css:17–219`; `settingsStyles.ts:1–71` (subset of token aliases); hardcoded overrides in `Users.tsx`, `Roles.tsx`, `Groups.tsx`, `OpcSources.tsx`, `BulkUpdate.tsx` | `index.css` is the canonical token registry for all three | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | ~20 token references; hardcoded hex for alarm priority/quality badge colors, TrendPane series palette, published dot | ~15 token references; 9 hardcoded color values in shell components; 2 undefined tokens (`--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`) | ~15 token references including `--io-border-subtle`, `--io-success`, `--io-status-fg`; undefined `--io-text-on-accent` in `btnPrimary`; hex-alpha badge concatenation bug in OpcSources | All three use `var(--io-accent)`, `var(--io-border)`, `var(--io-text-primary)`, `var(--io-text-muted)`, `var(--io-surface)` family, `var(--io-success)`/`--io-warning`/`--io-danger` | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-bg` (A1) and `--io-text` (A2) now defined in `index.css`. Remaining: alarm priority badge colors hardcoded instead of using `--io-alarm-*` tokens; published dot `#10b981` ≠ `var(--io-success)` | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-surface-hover` (A3) and `--io-font-sans` (A4) now defined. Remaining: 9 hardcoded values bypass tokens; `#09090b` hardcoded for active IconBtn text instead of `var(--io-accent-foreground)` | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-text-on-accent` (A5) now defined. Remaining: hex-alpha CSS var concatenation bug (`${color}20`) renders OpcSources StatusBadge backgrounds as invalid; hardcoded modal overlay/shadow values in Users/Roles/Groups | All three had at least one undefined token — cleared by 2b token pass; all three still have hardcoded hex colors that bypass the token system | Each module referenced a different set of undefined tokens; token gaps resolved by 2b |
| **Notes** | Console quality badge colors (`#10B981`, `#F59E0B`, `#EF4444`) have no corresponding tokens; hardcoded is the only option for that sub-type | `DesignerGraphicsList` `SCOPE_COLORS`/`MODE_COLORS` are mostly hardcoded rgba; two of six entries use registered tokens: `SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"` and `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"` (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55) | OpcSources StatusBadge `${color}20` concatenation bug is a functional regression: backgrounds render as transparent in all browsers | — | — |

---

## Category 2 — Typography

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Inline-styles; no Console-specific CSS classes or shared typography component | Inline-styles; no module-local CSS; `FieldLabel` component in `DesignerRightPanel` is the only reusable label primitive in any Designer file | Inline-styles; no module-specific CSS; `settingsStyles.ts:labelStyle` exports a named label constant used across most pages | All three use inline-styles exclusively; no shared typography component exists across modules | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.css:179–218` (16 scale tokens + `--io-font-mono`); inline styles scattered across `index.tsx`, `PaneWrapper`, `ConsolePalette`, `PaneConfigModal`, `AlarmListPane` | `index.css:178–218` (same 16 tokens); `DesignerRightPanel:201–217` (`FieldLabel`); `DesignerModeTabs`, `DesignerToolbar`, `DesignerTabBar`, `DesignerStatusBar` | `index.css`; `index.tsx:198–224` (nav group/item); `SettingsPageLayout:36–55` (page title); `settingsStyles.ts:15–21` (labelStyle); `SettingsTabs:60–75` (tab labels) | `index.css` typography token scale is the reference for all three; none actually use it | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | Raw px integers 9–18px; `fontFamily: monospace` on alarm tag/time (not `--io-font-mono`); SubGroupLabel at 9px and PaneTypeBadge at 10px are below smallest token (11px) | Raw px integers 9–20px; `var(--io-font-mono)` used correctly for zoom input (only instance); `var(--io-font-sans)` referenced in zoom dropdown but undefined; RightPanel `FieldLabel`: 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em | Raw px integers 11–18px; bare `"monospace"` keyword in `Groups.tsx` (not `--io-font-mono`); `labelStyle`: 12px/500/`--io-text-secondary`; page title 18px/600; table headers 11px/600/uppercase | All three use raw pixel integer literals; none reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`); all inherit the browser sans-serif stack | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | Zero typography scale tokens used; `fontFamily: monospace` instead of `--io-font-mono`; SubGroupLabel 9px below minimum token; no font sizes map cleanly to token equivalents | Zero typography scale tokens used; `var(--io-font-sans)` undefined; five different `letterSpacing` values for uppercase labels within `DesignerRightPanel` alone (0em, 0.04em, 0.05em, 0.06em, 0.08em) | Nav group header 11px vs AppShell 10px (1px drift); three different `letterSpacing` values across semantically identical uppercase label elements (0.06em, 0.07em, 0.08em); bare `"monospace"` in `Groups.tsx` instead of `--io-font-mono` | All three: zero typography scale tokens used; all three use bare `monospace` or no font-family token for monospace contexts | — |
| **Notes** | None of the inline sizes map cleanly to token equivalents (e.g., 15px, 16px are not defined tokens) | `FieldLabel` (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em) is the only reusable typography primitive in any of the three modules | Typography is most internally consistent in Settings; deviations are minor relative to Console and Designer | — | — |

---

## Category 3 — Toolbars

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Module-local-component; single 48px horizontal header bar defined as inline JSX inside `ConsolePage` | Module-local-component; four stacked dedicated components (`DesignerModeTabs` 36px, `DesignerToolbar` 44px, `DesignerTabBar` 36px, `DesignerStatusBar` 28px) | **Absent.** Per-page action rows above tables are inline flexbox rows, not toolbars. No toolbar container, no icon-button strip found | N/A — Settings has no toolbars; Console and Designer both use module-local components with no shared toolbar primitive | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:1942–2826` (full header + tab strip); `index.tsx:247–466` (`LayoutPickerButton`) | `DesignerModeTabs:207–217`; `DesignerToolbar:1159–1169`; `DesignerTabBar:363–376`; `DesignerStatusBar:192–203` | N/A | — | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | 48px height; `background: var(--io-surface)`; `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`; active tab: `borderBottom: 2px solid var(--io-accent)`; tab labels 13px/600 active/400 inactive; separator dividers `width:1, height:18, background: var(--io-border)` | DesignerToolbar 44px: same bg/border pattern; `IconBtn` 32×32 `var(--io-radius)`, `transition: background 0.1s, color 0.1s`; mode/tab strips with 2px accent underline; StatusBar 28px at bottom with segment dividers | N/A | Console + Designer share: `background: var(--io-surface)`, `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, active tab underline `2px solid var(--io-accent)`, tab labels font-weight active/inactive distinction | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | `var(--io-surface)` background where app shell topbar uses `var(--io-surface-primary)` (darker); none of 6 `--io-btn-*` tokens used; no toolbar height token; Publish button rendered twice in right controls block (duplicate render) | Toolbar text action buttons use `borderRadius: 6` integer while IconBtn in the same component uses `var(--io-radius)` (both 6px currently but inconsistent); all heights hardcoded (36/44/36/28); loading skeleton uses 40px instead of actual 44px | N/A | Console + Designer: all toolbar heights are hardcoded integers with no token | — |
| **Notes** | `LayoutPickerButton` is module-local with its own custom inline absolute-positioned dropdown (`zIndex: 200`) | Loading skeleton toolbar placeholder uses 40px height vs the actual 44px, producing a brief layout shift on load | N/A | — | — |

---

## Category 4 — Menus

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Implementation** | Mix; right-click context menus use `shared/components/ContextMenu`; export quick-format dropdown is a hand-rolled inline-positioned panel | Mix; three patterns: custom inline dropdown (File menu, `position: absolute`), custom fixed-position dropdown (zoom presets, `getBoundingClientRect`), shared `ContextMenu` (tab right-click) | Shared-component only; `ContextMenu` is the sole menu type; no custom dropdown panels | All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` for right-click context menus | — |
| **Source-of-truth files** | `index.tsx:3108–3405` (right-click menus); `index.tsx:2591–2654` (export dropdown); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `DesignerModeTabs:317–389` (File menu); `DesignerToolbar:1373–1469` (zoom dropdown); `DesignerTabBar:102–253` (tab context menu); `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx`; `Users:1628–1660`, `Roles`, `Groups` | `shared/components/ContextMenu.tsx` | — |
| **Visual properties actually applied** | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; Export dropdown: `position: absolute`, `background: var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: 6`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`, items 13px | ContextMenu: delegated to shared component; File menu: `position: absolute`, `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `boxShadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)`, `zIndex: 300`; Zoom dropdown: `position: fixed` (anchored via `getBoundingClientRect`), `zIndex: 2000` | ContextMenu container: `var(--io-surface-elevated)`, `border: 1px solid var(--io-border)`, `borderRadius: var(--io-radius)`, `boxShadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)`, `zIndex: 1800`; items: `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger (not `--io-danger`) | All three: ContextMenu uses `var(--io-surface-elevated)` bg, `var(--io-border)` border, `var(--io-radius)` radius; hover applied via imperative `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` DOM mutation | — |
| **Deviations from app shell** | Export dropdown uses direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.background`) for hover rather than shared `ContextMenu`; no CSS class or React state | **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-surface-hover` (A3) defined as alias for `var(--io-surface-elevated)`; `--io-font-sans` (A4) defined with full sans-serif font stack — zoom dropdown hover and font tokens now resolve. Remaining: box-shadows differ between File menu and zoom dropdown and do not use `--io-shadow-lg`; `zIndex: 300` vs `zIndex: 2000` across two custom dropdowns | None; Settings consumes `ContextMenu` identically to other modules | All three: custom dropdown implementations (where present) do not use `--io-shadow-lg` token | ContextMenu uses `var(--io-alarm-urgent)` for danger items rather than `var(--io-danger)`; same numeric value in dark theme but diverges in light theme |
| **Notes** | Export dropdown backdrop uses `position: fixed` — safe here since toolbar is not inside an RGL transform ancestor | File menu has no Escape key handler; Zoom dropdown uses `position: fixed` to escape toolbar's potential transform context (consistent with CLAUDE.md invariant) | `ConfirmDialog` (shared component) is used correctly for destructive actions; no dropdown menus needed | — | — |

---

## Category 5 — Side Panels

| Field | Console | Designer | Settings | Shared-across-all-three | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

This file is intentionally non-token-driven for ISA-101 compliance. |

## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules

1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`. **Note 2026-05-28 (Claim B):** `shared/styles/buttons.ts` `btnPrimary` now correctly uses `var(--io-accent-foreground)`; hardcoded values persist in individual files until consumer migration pass.

---

## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules

1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile. **Partially fixed 2026-05-28 (Claim B):** Console 3 inline modals, `RestorePreviewModal.tsx`, `TabClosePrompt.tsx`, `IographicExportDialog.tsx` migrated to Dialog wrapper (Radix provides ARIA automatically). Remaining: `Import` Modal/Drawer, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal`, designer wizard dialogs.
7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29). **Fixed 2026-05-27 (Claim A / 2b):** All tokens enumerated above are now defined — Console: `--io-bg` (A1), `--io-text` (A2); Designer: `--io-surface-hover` (A3), `--io-font-sans` (A4), `--io-error` (A6), `--io-surface-raised` (A7); `--io-accent-muted` not defined — single consumer `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` updated to use `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (A8); Settings: `--io-text-on-accent` (A5), `--io-overlay` (A9), `--io-accent-rgb` (A10). As of 2026-05-27, zero undefined token references exist among the tokens surveyed in this audit.
8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — **Fixed 2026-05-27 (2b):** `--io-z-modal` token raised from 300 to 1000; full z-index scale set (A13: dropdown:500, modal:1000, command:1200, visual-lock:1500, kiosk-auth:1800, toast:2000, emergency:3000). **Partially fixed 2026-05-28 (Claim B):** `ConfirmDialog.tsx` patched to `var(--io-z-modal)` / `calc(var(--io-z-modal) + 1)`; new `Dialog.tsx` consumers use `var(--io-modal-backdrop)`. Remaining hardcoded z-index values in unmigrated dialogs still uncoordinated — full migration deferred to consumer migration pass.
12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.

## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on

1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.

2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.

3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.

4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.

5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.

6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.

7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.

---

## Reconciliation Log

*Per-module audit files (01-console.md, 01-designer.md, 01-settings.md) not updated; 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections below apply to 02-comparison.md only.*

### Original Discrepancies — 03-verification.md

1. **Cat 1, Notes, Designer** — Removed "fully hardcoded" claim for SCOPE_COLORS/MODE_COLORS; two of six entries use registered tokens (`SCOPE_COLORS.console.text = "var(--io-accent)"`, `MODE_COLORS.report.text = "var(--io-text-secondary)"`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #1 (DesignerGraphicsList.tsx:48,55).

2. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Settings** — Removed "uppercase/0.05em" from labelStyle description; settingsStyles.ts:15-21 has neither `textTransform: "uppercase"` nor `letterSpacing`. Replaced with `marginBottom: 5px` and file citation. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #2.

3. **Cat 7, Visual Properties, Designer** — Removed "(unregistered token)" from `--io-input-bg`; added `--io-input-border` entry with registration citations (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 (ShapePointSelector.tsx:366-367).

4. **Cat 7, Deviations, Designer** — Replaced "appear to be unregistered" for `--io-input-bg`/`--io-input-border` with confirmed-registered status and line references (index.css:131-132). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3.

5. **Cat 7, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-space-*` appear unregistered" with confirmation that 17 tokens are defined at index.css:148-164. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #4.

6. **Cat 7, Implementation, Shared column** — Changed "none use the 5 `--io-input-*` tokens" to note that Designer's ShapePointSelector uses two of the five (`--io-input-bg`, `--io-input-border`). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / List 1 Item 7 correction.

7. **Cat 7, Deviations, Shared column** — Changed "All three: none use `--io-input-*` tokens" to accurate per-module split (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / Cat 7 table rows 4 and 8.

8. **Cat 8, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-info` undefined" with confirmation that `--io-info: #3b82f6` is defined at index.css:72; fallback equals token value, no visual regression. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #5 (AuthProviders.tsx:528).

9. **Cat 10, Notes, Designer** — Replaced "`io-multiselect-active` has no observable CSS rule" with confirmation that rule exists at index.css:904-909 (drop-shadow filter on selected nodes during multiselect). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #6.

10. **Cat 11, Visual Properties, Settings** — Changed `--io-modal-backdrop` label from "(undefined)" to "(defined: `var(--io-surface-overlay)` at index.css:139)". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8.

11. **Cat 11, Deviations, Settings** — Removed false "undefined" claim for `--io-modal-backdrop`; noted it is defined and used in ≥6 Settings files; corrected `window.confirm()` count from 4 to 8 with file breakdown (OpcSources:3, Import:3, CameraStreams:1, SupplementalConnectorsTab:1). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #7 and #8.

12. **Cat 11, Notes, Shared column** — Changed "All three: `--io-modal-backdrop` and `--io-modal-bg` tokens are not used" to accurately note `--io-modal-backdrop` IS used in ≥6 Settings files; only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8 / List 1 Item 11 correction.

13. **Cat 11, Implementation, Settings** — Updated `window.confirm()` count in implementation row from "4" to "8". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #7.

14. **List 1, Item 7** — Changed "All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-*` tokens" to partial-use statement (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 7 correction.

15. **List 1, Item 11** — Changed "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`" to accurate statement: only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 11 correction.

16. **List 2, Item 7, Settings undefined tokens** — Removed four tokens from Settings undefined list that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (discrepancy #8, index.css:139), `--io-info` (discrepancy #9, index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (discrepancy #10, index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (discrepancy #11, index.css:29). Retained genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #8–#11.

### Additional Discrepancies — 03c-pre-reconciliation.md, Section 3

17. **03c Discrepancy A — `--io-surface-sunken`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:26. Reviewed 02-comparison.md — no hedged language about `--io-surface-sunken` was present in the comparison file; it was already treated as a valid token. No text change to 02. The underlying inconsistency finding (Cat 5, Deviations, Designer: equipment tiles use `--io-surface-sunken` vs `--io-surface-elevated` for other tiles) remains a valid real inconsistency in surface-tier choice. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy A.

18. **03c Discrepancy B — `--io-accent-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:42 (`rgba(45,212,191,0.1)`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-accent-subtle` does not appear in the comparison file; hedged claims were only in 01-designer.md. No text change to 02. The CanvasLayerRow indigo fallback (`rgba(99,102,241,0.1)`) is dead code with a wrong hue — latent but not breaking. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy B.

19. **03c Discrepancy C — `--io-border-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:46 (`#27272a`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-border-subtle` appears in Cat 1 Visual Properties (Settings) already treated as a valid token reference. No hedged language present. No text change to 02. ### Shared Infrastructure Additions — 03c Section 2 + 03-verification.md Missing-from-Comparison

1	# Claim C Deferral Record
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-05-27
4	**Status:** Deferred — do not work on during regression, Claim A, or Claim B workstreams
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Section 1 — Scope of Deferral
9	
10	Claim C is the innermost of three concentric rectangles in the working architecture model: the canvas and work-surface layer where rendering happens. This deferral covers:
11	
12	- **`SceneRenderer`** and directly related shared rendering files — the graphics rendering engine consumed inside Console panes to render SVG display elements, alarm states, and LOD visibility
13	- **Shared graphics CSS infrastructure** — four files in `frontend/src/shared/graphics/` and `frontend/src/shared/clipboard/selection/`:
14	  - `selection.css` — CSS selection-box outline and glow applied to `.io-selection-overlay` children via `data-indicator` attribute
15	  - `alarmFlash.css` — CSS `@keyframes` alarm flash animations applied by SceneRenderer to SVG symbol instance wrappers
16	  - `operationalState.css` — ISA-101 operational state classes with intentionally hardcoded colors applied by SceneRenderer
17	  - `lod.css` — Level-of-Detail visibility rules applied via `lod-0` through `lod-3` classes on the canvas container
18	- **`WorkspaceGrid.tsx`** (Console) — the react-grid-layout tile dashboard container; the work-surface shell for Console panes
19	- **`DesignerCanvas.tsx`** (Designer) — the 12,067-line SVG editor with FSM interaction model (`interactionRef` Mode B); the work-surface for Designer
20	
21	The recommendations doc (04-recommendations.md, Category 10) confirms this distinction:
22	
23	> "Module-specific canvas containers are correct and should not be merged. Shared graphics infrastructure should be the focus."
24	> "The shared rendering engine exists and should be maintained. The work-surface container is intentionally module-specific."
25	
26	**Architectural distinction to preserve:** The shared rendering layer (SceneRenderer, the four CSS files) is already correctly shared and is not being changed. The module-specific container layer (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) serves fundamentally different work modes — a grid-layout tile dashboard vs. a 12,067-line SVG editor with FSM interactions — and merging them would introduce false coupling. Both layers are deferred, for different reasons: the shared layer because it already works correctly and its remaining imperfections depend on middle-rectangle work settling first; the container layer because convergence is architecturally incorrect.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Section 2 — Rationale for Deferral
31	
32	**First: Claim A and Claim B work will surface new requirements on the canvas layer.** Claim A (token registry, app shell uniformity) and Claim B (shared style constants, shared primitives: `FieldLabel`, `StatusBadge`, `Dialog`) will establish the token graph and the component surface that the canvas layer depends on. The Cat 10 imperfections in `WorkspaceGrid` and `DesignerCanvas` are mostly undefined-token references (e.g., `var(--io-bg)`, `var(--io-error)`) and hardcoded hex values — once Phase 1 token work lands, several of these resolve automatically via alias. Doing Claim C work before those aliases exist would require a second pass through the same files after Claim A lands.
33	
34	**Second: The seam between the rendering layer and module-specific containers will look different after middle-rectangle convergence.** The known imperfections in the canvas layer (canvas border token, resize handle color, guide line colors, DesignerCanvas context-menu token references) sit precisely at the junction between the rendering engine and the module-specific container. The middle-rectangle work (Claim B component promotion, dialog standardization, button and input constant files) will clarify which conventions cross that boundary and which remain local. Fixing the seam now means fixing it against a convention that is still in flux.
35	
36	**Third: The user's stated priority is uniformity in the outer and middle rectangles, not the canvas layer.** The outer rectangle (Claim A: token registry, sidebar, routing, theme) and the middle rectangle (Claim B: shared style constants and a small set of promoted components) are where inconsistency is most visible and most impactful across all three modules. The canvas layer's imperfections are largely invisible in normal use (dark theme only, undefined tokens that fall back gracefully) or are contained to their module. The regression exceptions (see Section 4) are bugs and are handled separately.
37	
38	---
39	
40	## Section 3 — Known Imperfections to Revisit
41	
42	Every concern from `04-recommendations.md` that touches the canvas or work-surface layer, in priority order. Fixes are not proposed here; this is a forward-looking inventory only.
43	
44	### 3.1 `alarmFlash.css` — hardcoded alarm hex colors
45	
46	**What:** Ten keyframe animations hardcode alarm colors as hex literals (`#ef4444`, `#f97316`, `#eab308`, `#f4f4f5`, `#60a5fa`). Values happen to match dark-theme token values but do not adapt to light or HPHMI themes.
47	
48	**Where:** `frontend/src/shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css:1-149` (Cat 8 primary, Cat 10 secondary in 02-comparison.md).
49	
50	**Category:** Functional (breaks theme adaptation for light/HPHMI themes; dark-theme output is currently correct).
51	
52	---
53	
54	### 3.2 Console `WorkspaceGrid` — undefined `var(--io-bg)` token
55	
56	**What:** The grid container background inherits `var(--io-bg)`, which is undefined. The Cat 1 Phase 1 token work will add `--io-bg` as an alias for `--io-surface-primary`, at which point this resolves automatically without touching `WorkspaceGrid.tsx`.
57	
58	**Where:** `WorkspaceGrid.tsx` container (Cat 10, Console Deviations in 02-comparison.md).
59	
60	**Category:** Cosmetic (falls back to transparent/inherited background; no visible breakage in current dark theme).
61	
62	---
63	
64	### 3.3 DesignerCanvas — canvas border hardcoded rgba
65	
66	**What:** The canvas border is `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` hardcoded. Should use a token or `var(--io-border)` with opacity.
67	
68	**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx:7452–12067` outer container (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).
69	
70	**Category:** Cosmetic (border is subtle; dark-theme output is acceptable; light theme would be incorrect).
71	
72	---
73	
74	### 3.4 DesignerCanvas — resize handles `fill="white"`
75	
76	**What:** SVG resize handles use `fill="white"` hardcoded. Should use `fill="var(--io-text-inverse)"` for theme-correctness.
77	
78	**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` resize handle elements (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).
79	
80	**Category:** Cosmetic (dark-theme output correct; light theme would render white handles on white background).
81	
82	---
83	
84	### 3.5 DesignerCanvas — `--io-error` undefined token references
85	
86	**What:** Context menu destructive item references `--io-error`, which is not defined. The correct token is `--io-danger`. The Cat 1 Phase 1 token work will add `--io-error` as an alias for `--io-danger`, resolving this automatically.
87	
88	**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` context menu (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md; also Cat 11).
89	
90	**Category:** Cosmetic (falls back to browser default or uncolored text; danger labeling is lost but not invisible).
91	
92	---
93	
94	### 3.6 DesignerCanvas — guide line colors hardcoded rgba
95	
96	**What:** Guide lines use `rgba(0,200,255,0.5)` (blue) and `rgba(255,160,0,0.5)` (orange) hardcoded. 04-recommendations.md notes these as "low priority, canvas-only; acceptable as-is if guide colors are intentional design choices."
97	
98	**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` guide line rendering (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).
99	
100	**Category:** Cosmetic (intentional design palette; does not affect theme correctness materially).
101	
102	---
103	
104	### 3.7 DesignerCanvas — grid lines fully hardcoded rgba
105	
106	**What:** Canvas grid lines use `rgba(128,128,128,0.12)` and `rgba(128,128,128,0.28)` hardcoded for normal and emphasis lines. No token equivalent.
107	
108	**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` grid line rendering (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).
109	
110	**Category:** Cosmetic (acceptable as-is per the recommendations; neutral gray values work in both dark and moderate-light contexts).
111	
112	---
113	
114	### 3.8 DesignerCanvas — "Paste as…" submenu items always disabled
115	
116	**What:** The "Paste as table" and "Paste as temporary-graphic" submenu items in the canvas context menu always render in a disabled state, regardless of clipboard contents.
117	
118	**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` context menu (Cat 10 Notes in 02-comparison.md; 04-recommendations.md notes it as "separate bug, out of scope for UI consistency work but worth a task file").
119	
120	**Category:** Functional (user-facing interaction bug; context menu items are always inaccessible).
121	
122	---
123	
124	## Section 4 — Exclusion List for the Next Workstreams
125	
126	The following files and scopes are **off-limits** during the regression workstream, Claim A workstream, and Claim B workstream because they belong to Claim C.
127	
128	### Files — do not touch
129	
130	| File | Path | Reason |
131	|------|------|--------|
132	| SceneRenderer | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/SceneRenderer.tsx` (and directly related files) | Core of the shared rendering engine; no pending Claim A/B actions |
133	| `alarmFlash.css` | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css` | Claim C imperfection; token migration is a Claim C action |
134	| `operationalState.css` | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/operationalState.css` | Intentional ISA-101 exception; documented do-not-touch |
135	| `lod.css` | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/lod.css` | No deviations; structural-only; Claim C exclusively |
136	| `WorkspaceGrid.tsx` | `frontend/src/console/WorkspaceGrid.tsx` (and `WorkspaceGrid.css`) | Module-specific container; Claim C scope |
137	| `DesignerCanvas.tsx` | `frontend/src/designer/DesignerCanvas.tsx` | Module-specific container; high-risk 12,067-line file; Claim C scope |
138	
139	### Audit categories — do not introduce new changes
140	
141	- **Category 10 entries in `02-comparison.md`** — all module-level and shared-infrastructure entries in Cat 10 are Claim C scope. The comparison file should not be updated with new Cat 10 findings or resolutions until Claim C is opened.
142	
143	### Important exception — regression fixes are NOT deferred
144	
145	Two functional regressions identified in `04-recommendations.md` touch files in the Claim C layer but are explicitly **not deferred**. They are bugs to fix during the regression workstream:
146	
147	1. **`var(--accent)` prefix bug** (`selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`): `var(--accent)` → `var(--io-accent)` in two files; `rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` → `var(--io-accent-subtle)` in `MarqueeLayer.tsx`. The selection highlight is currently invisible. This is a two-line fix with outsized impact. Although `selection.css` lives in the Claim C layer, the regression itself is a defect, not a Claim C architectural action. Fix it now; do not let this deferral block the regression workstream.
148	
149	2. **`OpcSources StatusBadge` hex-alpha concat bug**: `${color}20` string concatenation produces malformed color values. Fix via `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)` or early migration to `shared/components/StatusBadge`. This bug lives in Settings, not the canvas layer, but is listed here for completeness because it appears alongside the selection regression in the Phase 2 regression list.
150	
151	---
152	
153	## Section 5 — Trigger Conditions for Revisiting Claim C
154	
155	Claim C work should be reopened when all of the following conditions are met:
156	
157	1. **Claim A workstream complete** — including its check-in review. The `index.css` token registry has been updated with all missing tokens (`--io-bg`, `--io-text`, `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-error`, `--io-alarm-inactive`, `--io-text-inverse`). All three modules consume the canonical token set without undefined references.
158	
159	2. **Claim B workstream complete** — including its check-in review. `shared/styles/buttons.ts` and `shared/styles/inputs.ts` exist. `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx`, `shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx`, and `shared/components/Dialog.tsx` are promoted and in use. The middle-rectangle convergence is stable.
160	
161	3. **`04-recommendations.md` and `02-comparison.md` updated** to reflect what landed in Claim A and Claim B. The imperfections resolved automatically (via token aliases) should be noted as closed. The remaining open items in Cat 10 should be the confirmed scope of Claim C work.
162	
163	Once these conditions are met, reopen Claim C with the goal of: fixing the `alarmFlash.css` token migration (functional for light/HPHMI theme support), resolving remaining DesignerCanvas hardcoded values (canvas border, resize handles, guide lines) in a single minimally-invasive pass through that file, filing a task for the "Paste as…" submenu bug, and aligning the rendering-layer UX with the converged outer-and-middle-rectangle UX.

## Section 3 — Known Imperfections to Revisit

Every concern from `04-recommendations.md` that touches the canvas or work-surface layer, in priority order. Fixes are not proposed here; this is a forward-looking inventory only.

### 3.1 `alarmFlash.css` — hardcoded alarm hex colors

**What:** Ten keyframe animations hardcode alarm colors as hex literals (`#ef4444`, `#f97316`, `#eab308`, `#f4f4f5`, `#60a5fa`). Values happen to match dark-theme token values but do not adapt to light or HPHMI themes.

**Where:** `frontend/src/shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css:1-149` (Cat 8 primary, Cat 10 secondary in 02-comparison.md).

**Category:** Functional (breaks theme adaptation for light/HPHMI themes; dark-theme output is currently correct).

---

### 3.2 Console `WorkspaceGrid` — undefined `var(--io-bg)` token

**What:** The grid container background inherits `var(--io-bg)`, which is undefined. The Cat 1 Phase 1 token work will add `--io-bg` as an alias for `--io-surface-primary`, at which point this resolves automatically without touching `WorkspaceGrid.tsx`.

**Where:** `WorkspaceGrid.tsx` container (Cat 10, Console Deviations in 02-comparison.md).

**Category:** Cosmetic (falls back to transparent/inherited background; no visible breakage in current dark theme).

---

### 3.3 DesignerCanvas — canvas border hardcoded rgba

**What:** The canvas border is `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` hardcoded. Should use a token or `var(--io-border)` with opacity.

**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx:7452–12067` outer container (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).

**Category:** Cosmetic (border is subtle; dark-theme output is acceptable; light theme would be incorrect).

---

### 3.4 DesignerCanvas — resize handles `fill="white"`

**What:** SVG resize handles use `fill="white"` hardcoded. Should use `fill="var(--io-text-inverse)"` for theme-correctness.

**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` resize handle elements (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).

**Category:** Cosmetic (dark-theme output correct; light theme would render white handles on white background).

---

### 3.5 DesignerCanvas — `--io-error` undefined token references

**What:** Context menu destructive item references `--io-error`, which is not defined. The correct token is `--io-danger`. The Cat 1 Phase 1 token work will add `--io-error` as an alias for `--io-danger`, resolving this automatically.

**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` context menu (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md; also Cat 11).

**Category:** Cosmetic (falls back to browser default or uncolored text; danger labeling is lost but not invisible).

---

### 3.6 DesignerCanvas — guide line colors hardcoded rgba

**What:** Guide lines use `rgba(0,200,255,0.5)` (blue) and `rgba(255,160,0,0.5)` (orange) hardcoded. 04-recommendations.md notes these as "low priority, canvas-only; acceptable as-is if guide colors are intentional design choices."

**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` guide line rendering (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).

**Category:** Cosmetic (intentional design palette; does not affect theme correctness materially).

---

### 3.7 DesignerCanvas — grid lines fully hardcoded rgba

**What:** Canvas grid lines use `rgba(128,128,128,0.12)` and `rgba(128,128,128,0.28)` hardcoded for normal and emphasis lines. No token equivalent.

**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` grid line rendering (Cat 10, Designer Deviations in 02-comparison.md).

**Category:** Cosmetic (acceptable as-is per the recommendations; neutral gray values work in both dark and moderate-light contexts).

---

### 3.8 DesignerCanvas — "Paste as…" submenu items always disabled

**What:** The "Paste as table" and "Paste as temporary-graphic" submenu items in the canvas context menu always render in a disabled state, regardless of clipboard contents.

**Where:** `DesignerCanvas.tsx` context menu (Cat 10 Notes in 02-comparison.md; 04-recommendations.md notes it as "separate bug, out of scope for UI consistency work but worth a task file").

**Category:** Functional (user-facing interaction bug; context menu items are always inaccessible).

---

## Section 4 — Exclusion List for the Next Workstreams

The following files and scopes are **off-limits** during the regression workstream, Claim A workstream, and Claim B workstream because they belong to Claim C.

### Files — do not touch

| File | Path | Reason |
|------|------|--------|
| SceneRenderer | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/SceneRenderer.tsx` (and directly related files) | Core of the shared rendering engine; no pending Claim A/B actions |
| `alarmFlash.css` | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/alarmFlash.css` | Claim C imperfection; token migration is a Claim C action |
| `operationalState.css` | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/operationalState.css` | Intentional ISA-101 exception; documented do-not-touch |
| `lod.css` | `frontend/src/shared/graphics/lod.css` | No deviations; structural-only; Claim C exclusively |
| `WorkspaceGrid.tsx` | `frontend/src/console/WorkspaceGrid.tsx` (and `WorkspaceGrid.css`) | Module-specific container; Claim C scope |
| `DesignerCanvas.tsx` | `frontend/src/designer/DesignerCanvas.tsx` | Module-specific container; high-risk 12,067-line file; Claim C scope |

### Audit categories — do not introduce new changes

- **Category 10 entries in `02-comparison.md`** — all module-level and shared-infrastructure entries in Cat 10 are Claim C scope. The comparison file should not be updated with new Cat 10 findings or resolutions until Claim C is opened.

### Important exception — regression fixes are NOT deferred

Two functional regressions identified in `04-recommendations.md` touch files in the Claim C layer but are explicitly **not deferred**. They are bugs to fix during the regression workstream:

1. **`var(--accent)` prefix bug** (`selection.css` + `MarqueeLayer.tsx`): `var(--accent)` → `var(--io-accent)` in two files; `rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` → `var(--io-accent-subtle)` in `MarqueeLayer.tsx`. The selection highlight is currently invisible. This is a two-line fix with outsized impact. Although `selection.css` lives in the Claim C layer, the regression itself is a defect, not a Claim C architectural action. Fix it now; do not let this deferral block the regression workstream.

2. **`OpcSources StatusBadge` hex-alpha concat bug**: `${color}20` string concatenation produces malformed color values. Fix via `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)` or early migration to `shared/components/StatusBadge`. This bug lives in Settings, not the canvas layer, but is listed here for completeness because it appears alongside the selection regression in the Phase 2 regression list.

---

## Section 5 — Trigger Conditions for Revisiting Claim C

Claim C work should be reopened when all of the following conditions are met:

1. **Claim A workstream complete** — including its check-in review. The `index.css` token registry has been updated with all missing tokens (`--io-bg`, `--io-text`, `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-error`, `--io-alarm-inactive`, `--io-text-inverse`). All three modules consume the canonical token set without undefined references.

2. **Claim B workstream complete** — including its check-in review. `shared/styles/buttons.ts` and `shared/styles/inputs.ts` exist. `shared/components/FieldLabel.tsx`, `shared/components/StatusBadge.tsx`, and `shared/components/Dialog.tsx` are promoted and in use. The middle-rectangle convergence is stable.

3. **`04-recommendations.md` and `02-comparison.md` updated** to reflect what landed in Claim A and Claim B. The imperfections resolved automatically (via token aliases) should be noted as closed. The remaining open items in Cat 10 should be the confirmed scope of Claim C work.

Once these conditions are met, reopen Claim C with the goal of: fixing the `alarmFlash.css` token migration (functional for light/HPHMI theme support), resolving remaining DesignerCanvas hardcoded values (canvas border, resize handles, guide lines) in a single minimally-invasive pass through that file, filing a task for the "Paste as…" submenu bug, and aligning the rendering-layer UX with the converged outer-and-middle-rectangle UX.

Defining them now means Claim C can reference them immediately without re-entering the token layer.
175	
176	- **A6 (`--io-error` alias) and A1 (`--io-bg` alias) resolve Claim C imperfections automatically.** Once the aliases exist in `index.css`, DesignerCanvas's context menu destructive color (05 Section 3.5) and WorkspaceGrid's container background (05 Section 3.2) start rendering correctly without any code change in those Claim C files. This reduces the minimum touch-point count inside the high-risk 12,067-line `DesignerCanvas.tsx`.
177	
178	- **A13 (z-index scale) is a prerequisite for Claim C z-index work.** DesignerCanvas uses internal `zIndex` values in the 300–2000 range. When Claim C touches DesignerCanvas, those values must be coordinated with the scale established in A13. The scale decision is a dependency, not a blocker — Claim C should not begin its z-index work until A13 is decided and committed.
179	
180	- **A14 (sidebar width) does not directly affect the canvas seam.** The canvas containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) span the remaining viewport width after the sidebar. The canvas-to-sidebar boundary is clean regardless of which value (220px or 240px) is chosen, as long as the token and code are consistent.
181	
182	### 5.2 Conventions Established for the Eight-Module Rebuild
183	
184	The eight modules being rebuilt around the converged Console/Designer foundation will inherit the shell conventions locked in by Claim A. The following must be treated as non-negotiable constraints for all rebuilt modules:
185	
186	| Convention | Value | Applies to |
187	|---|---|---|
188	| Side panel background | `var(--io-surface-secondary)` | Any rebuilt module with a left or right panel. Designer's drift to `var(--io-surface)` is being corrected in B1 to establish this as the unambiguous standard. |
189	| Active nav item indicator | `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` + adjusted padding | Any rebuilt module with a sidebar nav list. |
190	| Sidebar width | `var(--io-sidebar-width)` (value per A14 decision) | All side panels in rebuilt modules must use this token, not a hardcoded integer. The value may not be known until A14 is decided — this token must be resolved before any rebuilt module's panel layout is coded. |
191	| Nav group header typography | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em / `var(--io-text-muted)` | All section-group labels within sidebar nav panels. |
192	| Toolbar surface | `var(--io-surface)` + `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | Module toolbars. This is the Console+Designer convention adopted in `04-recommendations.md` Cat 3 — not `var(--io-surface-primary)` (which is the AppShell reference but is not the adopted target for module toolbars). |
193	| Token hygiene | No token reference may be used that is not defined in `index.css` | Treat any undefined token reference in a rebuilt module as a blocking defect. The token list cleared by Claim A is the authoritative registry; nothing outside it may be referenced. |
194	
195	**One Claim A item must be resolved before any of the eight modules begins panel-layout work:** A14 (`--io-sidebar-width` value). Building any panel at a hardcoded width and later discovering the chosen value was different requires an eleven-module edit. Decide it once, enforce it everywhere.
196	
197	---
198	
199	## Section 6 — Lessons for Claim B
200	
201	Captured at Claim A close (2026-05-27). These are observations from execution that should shape how Claim B is planned and run.
202	
203	### L1 — "Defer" notes in a plan are hard gates, not suggestions
204	
205	A13 explicitly noted `--io-z-command` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth` as deferred to Claim B pending a cross-module z-index audit. The 2b implementer wired `CommandPalette.tsx` to use these tokens anyway — setting `--io-z-command: 1200` without the audit that was supposed to precede it. The values turned out directionally correct and were accepted, but the pattern is dangerous: the audit gate existed because an incorrect value is harder to fix after code has migrated to the token. **For Claim B:** any plan item with an explicit "defer" gate must be treated as blocked until that gate is cleared. If the gate is wrong, clear it first through an explicit decision, then proceed.
206	
207	### L2 — Token audit precision: verify "undefined" claims before writing fixes
208	
209	Plan item A12 claimed `--io-text-inverse` was not yet defined. It was already defined in all three theme blocks. The plan had a wrong claim that would have produced a duplicate definition if executed mechanically. **For Claim B:** before any "fix undefined token" task, grep `index.css` and the `tokens.ts` mirror to confirm the token is actually absent. Two-minute verification eliminates a class of bad commits.
210	
211	### L3 — Alias approach is lower-risk than code-replacement for Claim C files
212	
213	A7 (`--io-surface-raised`) was cited in the recommendations as "replace the reference in `CanvasLayerRow`." The plan instead defined the alias token. Both approaches reach the same visual outcome, but the alias approach touches zero component files — safer for tokens consumed inside high-risk files like `DesignerCanvas.tsx`. **For Claim B:** prefer alias approach for any token fix whose consumers include Claim C files. Reserve code-replacement for tokens whose consumers are in low-risk, fully-owned Claim B files.
214	
215	### L4 — Single-consumer tokens: fix the consumer, not the registry
216	
217	A8 (`--io-accent-muted`) had exactly one consumer (`PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168`) with a hardcoded fallback. Rather than defining a new token for one usage, the consumer was updated to use an existing token. **For Claim B:** before defining any new token, count consumers. If count is one and a semantically close existing token exists, fix at the consumer. New tokens should have ≥2 consumers or represent a genuinely new semantic concept.
218	
219	### L5 — Plan prose should describe mechanism, not visual effect
220	
221	The B2 plan entry said "reduce `paddingLeft` by 2px to maintain alignment." The correct implementation uses a uniform padding (`7px 10px 7px 8px`) with a transparent 2px border on inactive items — which is better than the described approach because it reserves space without state-conditional math. The plan described the visual effect rather than the CSS property values. The implementer caught the discrepancy and chose the better approach, but the ambiguity introduced unnecessary decision overhead. **For Claim B:** plan entries for CSS fixes should specify the exact property-value changes, not the intended visual outcome. "Set `borderLeft: 2px solid transparent` on inactive state; set `padding: 7px 10px 7px 8px` uniformly" is unambiguous. "Maintain alignment" is not.
222	
223	### L6 — DoD criteria must name specific grep scopes
224	
225	Criterion 1 referenced "shell-layer files" without defining which files constitute the shell layer. The implementer had to interpret this. **For Claim B DoD:** any criterion involving a grep check must name the scope explicitly — either "all files under `frontend/src/`" or an enumerated list of files. Ambiguous scope in DoD allows partial verification to masquerade as full verification.
226	
227	### L7 — Pre-execution token validation is a required first step
228	
229	Several A-items (A1–A12) required checking the current state of `index.css` before writing. A12 would have caused a regression if executed without that check. The plan's "research-gated" category acknowledged this for some items but not all. **For Claim B:** treat token validation (grep before write) as a mandatory first step for every token-touching task — even "obviously absent" tokens. Add it explicitly to the DoD of any token-registry task.
230
